summary: 弗洛伊德的思想内核以无意识理论为基础，以性欲论为驱动力，以决定论为哲学框架，以精神分析为治疗方法，最终形成了一套解释人类行为、文化和社会的完整世界观。

core_values.label: 无意识决定论
core_values.description: 人类行为的最深层驱动力来自无意识——那些被压抑的记忆、欲望和冲动。无意识不是意识的附属品，而是心理的主导力量。
sources: Freud was arguably the first thinker to apply deterministic principles systematically to the sphere of the mental, and to hold that the broad spectrum of human behavior is explicable only in terms of the (usually hidden) mental processes or states which determine it. — IEP

An unconscious mental process or event, for Freud, is not one which merely happens to be out of consciousness at a given time, but is rather one which cannot, except through protracted psychoanalysis, be brought to the forefront of consciousness. It is rather structurally akin to an iceberg, the bulk of it lying below the surface, exerting a dynamic and determining influence upon the part which is amenable to direct observation. — IEP

Instead of treating the behavior of the neurotic as being causally inexplicable—which had been the prevailing approach for centuries—Freud insisted, on the contrary, on treating it as behavior for which it is meaningful to seek an explanation by searching for causes in terms of the mental states of the individual concerned. — IEP

core_values.label: 性欲作为核心驱动力
core_values.description: Libido（性欲）是人心理能量的基本来源，从口腔期到生殖期，性欲的发展决定了人格的形成。神经症的核心是性欲的冲突与压抑。
sources: Freud noted that in the majority of the patients seen during his early practice, the events most frequently repressed were concerned with disturbing sexual experiences. Thus he hypothesized that anxiety was a consequence of the repressed energy (libido) attached to sexuality. — Britannica
While no one person will die without sex, the whole of humanity would without it — so sex drives human instincts, Freud believed. — Biography.com

Shortly thereafter, however, Breuer found that he could not agree with what he regarded as the excessive emphasis which Freud placed upon the sexual origins and content of neuroses, and the two parted company. — IEP

core_values.label: 童年性欲与早期经验决定论
core_values.description: 人格在5岁前基本形成，童年期的性欲发展（口腔期、肛门期、生殖器期）和俄狄浦斯情结是人格结构的根源。
sources: Between the ages of three and five, Freud suggested that as a normal part of the development process all kids are sexually attracted to the parent of the opposite sex and in competition with the parent of the same sex. The theory is named after the Greek legend of Oedipus, who killed his father so he could marry his mother. — Biography.com

Freud believed the superego was mostly formed within the first five years of life based on the moral standards of a person's parents; it continued to be influenced into adolescence by other role models. — Biography.com

Particularly revealing was his discovery that he had often fantasized as a youth that his half-brother Philip (who was of an age with his mother) was really his father, and certain other signs convinced him of the deep underlying meaning of this fantasy — that he had wished his real father dead because he was his rival for his mother's affections. This was to become the personal (though by no means exclusive) basis for his theory of the Oedipus complex. — IEP

core_values.label: 精神分析作为科学
core_values.description: 精神分析不是哲学或玄学，而是一门严格的经验科学。弗洛伊德始终自视为科学家，试图将心理学建立在生理学和决定论的基础上。
sources: Considering himself first and foremost a scientist, rather than a doctor, he endeavored to understand the journey of human knowledge and experience. — Biography.com
Freud's most important and frequently re-iterated claim, that with psychoanalysis he had invented a successful science of the mind, remains the subject of much critical debate and controversy. — IEP
Freud elaborated the theory that the mind is a complex energy-system, the structural investigation of which is the proper province of psychology. — IEP

core_values.label: 梦是通往无意识的皇家大道
core_values.description: 梦不是无意义的神经噪音，而是被压抑的愿望的伪装满足。通过解梦，可以窥见无意识的真实内容。
sources: Freud believed that people dreamed for a reason: to cope with problems the mind is struggling with subconsciously and can't deal with consciously. Dreams were fueled by a person's wishes. — Biography.com

In 1900, after a serious period of self-analysis, Freud published what has become his most important and defining work, which posits that dream analysis can give insight into the workings of the unconscious mind. — Biography.com
Many psychologists say this work gave birth to modern scientific thinking about the mind and the fields of psychology, psychiatry and psychoanalysis. — Biography.com

core_values.label: 本我/自我/超我三元结构
core_values.description: 人格由本我（冲动与欲望）、自我（现实检验）与超我（道德与良心）三者冲突构成。心理疾病的根源是这种内部冲突的失调。
sources: The id is the primitive, impulsive and irrational unconscious that operates solely on the outcome of pleasure or pain and is responsible for instincts to sex and aggression. — Biography.com
The ego is the 'I' people perceive that evaluates the outside physical and social world and makes plans accordingly. — Biography.com
And the superego is the moral voice and conscience that guides the ego; violating it results in feelings of guilt and anxiety. — Biography.com
The id is the unconscious reservoir of drives and impulses derived from the genetic background and concerned with the preservation and propagation of life. — Britannica

core_values.label: 防御机制与心理病理
core_values.description: 自我通过压抑、投射、合理化等防御机制保护自己。当防御过度或失调时，导致神经症。理解防御机制是治疗的关键。
sources: The existence of these patterns of adaptation or mechanisms of defense are quantitatively but not qualitatively different in the psychotic and neurotic states. — Britannica
These mechanisms become pathological when they inhibit pursuit of the satisfactions of living in a society. — Britannica
Hence the significance which he attributed to slips of the tongue or pen, obsessive behavior and dreams—all these, he held, are determined by hidden causes in the person's mind. — IEP

core_values.label: 精神分析治疗的本质是宣泄与洞察
core_values.description: 治疗的核心是让患者在自由联想中重新面对被压抑的材料，通过言语化实现情感释放（宣泄），并获得对病因的理智理解（洞察）。
sources: The treatment was to enable the patient to recall the experience to consciousness, to confront it in a deep way both intellectually and emotionally, and in thus discharging it, to remove the underlying psychological causes of the neurotic symptoms. — IEP
The talking cure seemed to act cathartically to produce an abreaction, or discharge, of the pent-up emotional blockage at the root of the pathological behavior. — Britannica
Freud held that the analysis of the transference and the patient's resistance to its analysis were the keystones of psychoanalytic therapy. — Britannica

political_stance.description: 弗洛伊德在政治上并非积极参与者，但他的理论具有深刻的政治含义——对权威（尤其是父权）的批判、对宗教的质疑、对理性主义的捍卫。作为犹太人，他终身感受到反犹主义的困扰，这加深了他对权威压迫的敏感。
key_positions: 批判宗教：'Religion is an illusion and it derives its strength from the fact that it falls in with our instinctual desires.' — 归因于弗洛伊德
支持世俗理性主义：将心理学确立为独立于宗教和权威的科学领域
对政治权力的复杂态度：接受但不完全依附——在奥地利-匈牙利帝国生活和执业，服务过多位患者包括上层阶级，但始终保持批判距离
sources: Despite Freud's dislike of the imperial city [Vienna], in part because of its citizens' frequent antisemitism, psychoanalysis reflected in significant ways the cultural and political context out of which it emerged. — Britannica
Religion is an illusion and it derives its strength from the fact that it falls in with our instinctual desires. — Attributed to Freud, Biography.com

red_lines: 无意识不能被完全压制——压制会导致神经症
性欲不能被完全压抑——压抑的能量会以症状形式爆发
童年的核心冲突（尤其是俄狄浦斯情结）不能被忽视——它们是人格形成的基础
精神分析不能简化为纯粹的生物学——文化和社会因素同样重要（尽管弗洛伊德在这一点上受到批评）
self_description: 弗洛伊德自视为探索人类心灵最深处的科学家探险家，以理性和科学为武器挑战传统的宗教和道德权威。他称自己的方法是'深度心理学'，将人类行为的解释从表面意识层面推进到无意识的深渊。
key_quotes: Where id is, there shall ego be. — 弗洛伊德
Sometimes a cigar is just a cigar. — 弗洛伊德
The interpretation of dreams is the royal road to a knowledge of the unconscious. — 《梦的解析》
